        "contents": "-- =============================================================================\n-- Integration Settings - Store Moco/Cituro etc. API keys and subdomains (UI config)\n-- =============================================================================\n\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS integration_settings (\n    name    VARCHAR(64) PRIMARY KEY,\n    payload JSONB       NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',\n    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n);\n\nCOMMENT ON TABLE integration_settings IS 'Per-integration config (e.g. moco: api_key_encrypted, subdomain) overridable via UI';",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "/**\n * System Config Settings\n * Displays routing configuration, scoring thresholds, and feature flags\n */\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport {\n  Box,\n  Typography,\n  Card,\n  CardContent,\n  Grid,\n  Chip,\n  CircularProgress,\n  Alert,\n  Divider,\n  Paper,\n  Table,\n  TableBody,\n  TableCell,\n  TableContainer,\n  TableHead,\n  TableRow,\n} from '@mui/material';\nimport {\n  Settings as SettingsIcon,\n  Score as ScoreIcon,\n  ToggleOn as ToggleOnIcon,\n  Info as InfoIcon,\n} from '@mui/icons-material';\nimport { getRoutingConfig, getScoringThresholds } from '../../providers/dataProvider';\n\nconst SystemConfig = () =&gt; {\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [error, setError] = useState(null);\n  const [routingConfig, setRoutingConfig] = useState(null);\n  const [scoringThresholds, setScoringThresholds] = useState(null);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    loadConfigData();\n  }, []);\n\n  const loadConfigData = async () =&gt; {\n    setLoading(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      const [routingData, thresholdsData] = await Promise.all([\n        getRoutingConfig(),\n        getScoringThresholds(),\n      ]);\n      setRoutingConfig(routingData);\n      setScoringThresholds(thresholdsData);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error('Error loading config data:', err);\n      setError(err.message || 'Failed to load configuration');\n    } finally {\n      setLoading(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  if (loading) {\n    return (\n      &lt;Box sx={{ display: 'flex', justifyContent: 'center', py: 8 }}&gt;\n        &lt;CircularProgress /&gt;\n      &lt;/Box&gt;\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (error) {\n    return (\n      &lt;Alert severity=\"error\" sx={{ mb: 2 }}&gt;\n        {error}\n      &lt;/Alert&gt;\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Score tier definitions with colors",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "/**\n * Settings Page - Tab-based Layout\n * Integrated with real Team Management API\n */\nimport { useState } from 'react';\nimport { useGetIdentity, useNotify } from 'react-admin';\nimport {\n  Box,\n  Typography,\n  Card,\n  CardContent,\n  Grid,\n  TextField,\n  Button,\n  Switch,\n  FormControlLabel,\n  Divider,\n  Avatar,\n  Chip,\n  Tabs,\n  Tab,\n  IconButton,\n  Popover,\n} from '@mui/material';\nimport {\n  Person as PersonIcon,\n  Notifications as NotificationsIcon,\n  People as PeopleIcon,\n  Palette as ThemeIcon,\n  Extension as IntegrationIcon,\n  Settings as SettingsIcon,\n  ViewKanban as PipelineIcon,\n} from '@mui/icons-material';\nimport AddPhotoAlternateIcon from '@mui/icons-material/AddPhotoAlternate';\nimport { API_URL } from '../../providers/dataProvider';\n\n// Avatar-Dateien aus /public/avatars (wie auf RegisterPage)\nconst AVATAR_FILES = (() =&gt; {\n  const list = [];\n  for (let row = 1; row &lt;= 5; row++) {\n    for (let col = 1; col &lt;= 9; col++) list.push(&#96;row-${row}-column-${col}.png&#96;);\n  }\n  for (let row = 6; row &lt;= 9; row++) {\n    for (let col = 5; col &lt;= 9; col++) list.push(&#96;row-${row}-column-${col}.png&#96;);\n  }\n  return list;\n})();\nimport TeamManagement from './TeamManagement';\nimport IntegrationSettings from './IntegrationSettings';\nimport SystemConfig from './SystemConfig';\nimport NotificationPreferences from './NotificationPreferences';\nimport { PipelineSettings } from '../Pipelines';\n\n// TabPanel component\nconst TabPanel = ({ children, value, index, ...other }) =&gt; {\n  return (\n    &lt;div\n      role=\"tabpanel\"\n      hidden={value !== index}\n      id={&#96;settings-tabpanel-${index}&#96;}\n      aria-labelledby={&#96;settings-tab-${index}&#96;}\n      {...other}\n    &gt;\n      {value === index &amp;&amp; &lt;Box sx={{ pt: 3 }}&gt;{children}&lt;/Box&gt;}\n    &lt;/div&gt;\n  );\n};\n\nconst ROLE_LABELS = {\n  admin: 'Administrator',",

        "contents": "// =============================================================================\n// Start Server\n// =============================================================================\n\nconst start = async () =&gt; {\n  try {\n    // Initialize database connection\n    fastify.log.info('Connecting to database...');\n    try {\n      const dbHealthy = await db.healthCheck();\n      if (!dbHealthy) {\n        throw new Error('Database health check returned false');\n      }\n    } catch (dbError) {\n      const err = dbError as Error;\n      fastify.log.error({ err, message: err.message }, 'Database connection error');\n      throw new Error(&#96;Failed to connect to database: ${err.message}&#96;);\n    }\n    fastify.log.info('✅ Database connected');\n\n    // Load DB-backed integration config (Moco, etc.) into memory\n    await loadIntegrationSettingsCache();\n    fastify.log.info('✅ Integration settings cache loaded');\n\n    // Initialize Redis connection\n    fastify.log.info('Connecting to Redis...');\n    getRedisConnection();\n    fastify.log.info('✅ Redis connected');\n\n    // Initialize BullMQ queues\n    fastify.log.info('Initializing queues...');\n    initializeQueues();\n    fastify.log.info('✅ Queues initialized');\n\n    // Start listening\n    await fastify.listen({ \n      port: config.port, \n      host: config.host \n    });\n\n    fastify.log.info(&#96;\n╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n║                                                           ║\n║   🧬 DNA Marketing Engine v1.0.0                          ║\n║                                                           ║\n║   Server running at http://\n\n;",

        "contents": "// src/db/index.ts\n// PostgreSQL Database Connection Pool\n// =============================================================================\n\nimport pg from 'pg';\nimport { config } from '../config/index.js';\nimport { DatabaseError } from '../errors/index.js';\n\nconst { Pool } = pg;\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Pool Configuration\n// =============================================================================\n\nconst poolConfig: pg.PoolConfig = {\n  connectionString: config.databaseUrl,\n  max: 20,                    // Maximum connections in pool\n  min: 2,                     // Minimum connections in pool\n  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,   // Close idle connections after 30s\n  connectionTimeoutMillis: 5000, // Connection timeout\n  allowExitOnIdle: false\n};\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Create Pool\n// =============================================================================\n\nlet pool: pg.Pool | null = null;\n\nexport function getPool(): pg.Pool {\n  if (!pool) {\n    pool = new Pool(poolConfig);\n\n    pool.on('connect', () =&gt; {\n      console.log('✅ PostgreSQL client connected');\n    });\n\n    pool.on('error', (err) =&gt; {\n      console.error('❌ PostgreSQL pool error:', err.message);\n    });\n\n    pool.on('remove', () =&gt; {\n      console.log('PostgreSQL client removed from pool');\n    });\n  }\n\n  return pool;\n}\n\n// =============================================================================",

        "contents": "const StyledRow = \n${({ theme }) =&gt; theme.spacing(2)};\n&#96;;\n\nconst StyledButtonContainer = styled.div&#96;\n  display: flex;\n  &amp; &gt; :not(:first-of-type) &gt; button {\n    border-left: none;\n  }\n&#96;;\n\nconst RESET_VARIABLE_MODAL_ID = 'reset-variable-modal';\n\nexport const SettingsAdminConfigVariableDetails = () =&gt; {\n  const { variableName } = useParams();\n  const { t } = useLingui();\n  const [isEditing, setIsEditing] = useState(false);\n  const { openModal } = useModal();\n  const isConfigVariablesInDbEnabled = useRecoilValue(\n    isConfigVariablesInDbEnabledState,\n  );\n\n  const { data: configVariableData, loading } =\n    useGetDatabaseConfigVariableQuery({\n      variables: { key: variableName ?? '' },\n      fetchPolicy: 'network-only',\n    });\n\n  const variable = configVariableData?.getDatabaseConfigVariable;\n\n  const { handleUpdateVariable, handleDeleteVariable } =\n    useConfigVariableActions(variable?.name ?? '');\n\n  const {\n    handleSubmit,\n    setValue,\n    isSubmitting,\n    watch,\n    hasValueChanged,\n    isValueValid,\n  } = useConfigVariableForm(variable);\n\n  if (loading === true || isDefined(variable) === false) {\n    return &lt;SettingsSkeletonLoader /&gt;;\n  }\n\n  const isEnvOnly = variable.isEnvOnly;\n  const isFromDatabase = variable.source === ConfigSource.DATABASE;\n\n  const onSubmit = async (formData: { value: ConfigVariableValue }) =&gt; {\n    await handleUpdateVariable(formData.value, isFromDatabase);\n    setIsEditing(false);\n  };\n\n  const handleEditClick = () =&gt; {\n    setIsEditing(true);\n  };\n\n  const handleXButtonClick = () =&gt; {\n    if (isFromDatabase &amp;&amp; hasValueChanged) {\n      setValue('value', variable.value);\n      setIsEditing(false);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (isFromDatabase &amp;&amp; !hasValueChanged) {\n      openModal(RESET_VARIABLE_MODAL_ID);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    setValue('value', variable.value);\n    setIsEditing(false);\n  };\n\n  const handleConfirmReset = () =&gt; {\n    handleDeleteVariable();\n    setIsEditing(false);\n  };\n\n  return (\n;",

          }
        },
        "contents": "/** Save Moco config (api_key optional; requires ENCRYPTION_KEY if saving api_key). */\nexport async function setMocoConfig(updates: { api_key?: string; subdomain?: string }): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\n  const current = await db.queryOne&lt;{ payload: MocoPayload }&gt;(\n    &#96;SELECT payload FROM integration_settings WHERE name = $1&#96;,\n    [MOCO_KEY]\n  );\n  const prev = (current?.payload as MocoPayload) || {};\n  const next: MocoPayload = { ...prev };\n\n  if (updates.subdomain !== undefined) next.subdomain = updates.subdomain.trim() || undefined;\n  if (updates.api_key !== undefined) {\n    if (updates.api_key.trim() === '') {\n      next.api_key_encrypted = undefined;\n    } else {\n      try {\n        next.api_key_encrypted = encrypt(updates.api_key.trim());\n      } catch (e) {\n        throw new Error(\n          'API-Key-Verschlüsselung fehlgeschlagen. Bitte ENCRYPTION_KEY in der .env setzen (64 Hex-Zeichen).'\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  await db.query(\n    &#96;INSERT INTO integration_settings (name, payload, updated_at)\n     VALUES ($1, $2, NOW())\n     ON CONFLICT (name) DO UPDATE SET payload = $2, updated_at = NOW()&#96;,\n    [MOCO_KEY, JSON.stringify(next)]\n  );\n  mocoCache = null;\n  await loadIntegrationSettingsCache();\n}\n\n// =============================================================================",

(\n        &lt;&gt;\n          &lt;Route path={SettingsPath.AdminPanel} element={&lt;SettingsAdmin /&gt;} /&gt;\n          &lt;Route\n            path={SettingsPath.AdminPanelIndicatorHealthStatus}\n            element={&lt;SettingsAdminIndicatorHealthStatus /&gt;}\n          /&gt;\n          &lt;Route\n            path={SettingsPath.AdminPanelQueueDetail}\n            element={&lt;SettingsAdminQueueDetail /&gt;}\n          /&gt;\n\n          &lt;Route\n            path={SettingsPath.AdminPanelConfigVariableDetails}\n            element={&lt;SettingsAdminConfigVariableDetails /&gt;}\n          /&gt;\n        &lt;/&gt;\n      )}\n\n      &lt;Route\n        element=\n;",

        "contents": "let mocoCache: { apiKey?: string; subdomain?: string } | null = null;\n\n/** Load integration_settings from DB into memory (call at startup / after PUT). */\nexport async function loadIntegrationSettingsCache(): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\n  try {\n    const row = await db.queryOne&lt;{ payload: MocoPayload }&gt;(\n      &#96;SELECT payload FROM integration_settings WHERE name = $1&#96;,\n      [MOCO_KEY]\n    );\n    if (!row?.payload) {\n      mocoCache = null;\n      return;\n    }\n    const p = row.payload as MocoPayload;\n    const out: { apiKey?: string; subdomain?: string } = {};\n    if (p.subdomain) out.subdomain = p.subdomain;\n    if (p.api_key_encrypted) {\n      try {\n        out.apiKey = decrypt(p.api_key_encrypted);\n      } catch {\n        // ignore invalid/legacy encrypted value\n      }\n    }\n    mocoCache = Object.keys(out).length ? out : null;\n  } catch {\n    // Table might not exist yet (migration not run)\n    mocoCache = null;\n  }\n}\n\n/** Sync: get Moco config (cache or env). Used by getMocoService(). */\nexport function getMocoRuntimeConfig(): { apiKey?: string; subdomain?: string } {\n  const fromEnv = {\n    apiKey: config.moco.apiKey ?? undefined\n  };\n}",

I'll create the admin route. For TRUNCATE we need to respect FK order when not using CASCADE, or we can use TRUNCATE with CASCADE. In PostgreSQL, TRUNCATE table1, table2, ... CASCADE will truncate the given tables and any tables that have foreign-key references to them. So we list the "top-level" tables we want to clear and CASCADE will clear dependent tables. But actually it's the opposite: CASCADE automatically truncates tables that have foreign key references TO the listed tables. So if we TRUNCATE leads CASCADE, it will also truncate intent_signals, score_history, deals, etc. So we need to list the "root" tables: leads, organizations, campaigns, pipelines (no - keep), pipeline_stages (keep), team_members (keep), scoring_rules (keep), automation_rules, integration_settings (keep?), api_keys (keep). So list: automation_rules, campaigns, organizations, leads. Then CASCADE will truncate: automation_logs, tasks, deals, score_history, intent_signals, events(?), conversations, messages, email_sequence_enrollments, email_tracking, email_sequences?, email_sequence_steps?. Actually leads references organizations and pipelines. So if we truncate leads CASCADE we get intent_signals, score_history, deals (deals also ref pipeline_stages), conversations, email_sequence_enrollments. Then we need to truncate automation_logs, tasks, messages (via conversations), automation_rules, campaigns, organizations. Order: 1) automation_logs (refs automation_rules, leads, deals), 2) tasks, 3) messages, 4) conversations, 5) email_tracking, 6) email_sequence_enrollments, 7) email_sequence_steps, 8) email_sequences, 9) automation_rules, 10) deals, 11) score_history, 12) intent_signals, 13) events, 14) leads, 15) campaigns, 16) organizations. Let me use explicit order and run multiple truncates, or one TRUNCATE with all tables. In PostgreSQL: TRUNCATE TABLE a, b, c ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE. If we list all 16 tables, CASCADE will truncate any other tables that have FK to any of these. So we might get duplicate table in CASCADE - no, CASCADE only adds tables that reference our list. So we list all data tables we want empty. Safe list: automation_logs, tasks, messages, conversations, email_tracking, email_sequence_enrollments, email_sequence_steps, email_sequences, automation_rules, deals, score_history, intent_signals, events, leads, campaigns, organizations. That's 16 tables. TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE. Let me check - does "events" need to be truncated as partition parent? Yes, TRUNCATE events will cascade to partitions. Good.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So in auth, they use request.jwtVerify() in the decorate and then request.user is used. So the JWT plugin must attach the decoded payload to request.user. Let me check - in @fastify/jwt, after jwtVerify the payload is available. I need to see how it's attached. Often it's request.user = payload. So for admin route we require JWT (we use authenticateOrApiKey - so API key could also pass). If API key passes, request.user might be undefined! So we should either: 1) Only allow JWT for this route (use a preHandler that only does jwtVerify, not API key), or 2) Reject when no user (API key users get 403). I'll keep authenticateOrApiKey and then require user with role admin - so API key callers will get "Only administrators can clear databases" because request.user won't be set. Good.

Now register admin routes and add the frontend. Also need to fix: db.transaction - let me check the db module for transaction API.
</details></think>

Кратко, что сделано:

### 1. Бэкенд

- **Файл:** `src/api/routes/admin.ts`  
  - Роут: `POST /api/v1/admin/clear-databases`.  
  - Доступ только для пользователей с ролью `admin` (проверка по JWT).  
  - В одной транзакции выполняется `TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE` по таблицам:  
    `automation_logs`, `tasks`, `messages`, `conversations`, `email_tracking`, `email_sequence_enrollments`, `email_sequence_steps`, `email_sequences`, `automation_rules`, `deals`, `score_history`, `intent_signals`, `events`, `leads`, `campaigns`, `organizations`.  
  - Не трогаются: `team_members`, `pipelines`, `pipeline_stages`, `scoring_rules`, `integration_settings`, `api_keys`.

- Роут подключён в `src/index.ts`.

### 2. Фронтенд

- **Провайдер:** в `dataProvider.js` добавлена функция `clearDatabases()` — отправляет `POST` на `/api/v1/admin/clear-databases` с текущим токеном.
- **Настройки:** в **Settings → System Config** добавлена карточка **«Очистка всех баз данных»** (видна только при `identity.role === 'admin'`):
  - кнопка **«Очистить все базы данных»**;
  - диалог подтверждения с предупреждением, что действие необратимо;
  - после успешного ответа API показывается уведомление и диалог закрывается.

Итог: в настройках (вкладка System Config) у администраторов есть кнопка очистки перечисленных баз данных с подтверждением; остальные роли этой карточки не видят.

---

_**User**_

а можно через дроп даун выбрать какие базхы данных как связваны схематично и что именно и как удалить. то есть сделав этот процесс более интерактивным чтобы таблицы оставались нормализованными и было пояснения о каскадном удалении.

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants to make the database clearing process more interactive:
1. A dropdown to select which databases/tables to clear
2. A schematic view of how the databases are connected (relationships)
3. Explanations of what will be deleted and how (cascade deletion)
4. Keep tables normalized and show cascade behavior

So I need to:
1. Backend: Provide an endpoint that returns the schema of clearable tables with their relationships (FK dependencies) and cascade info - so the frontend can show a graph/tree and let user select what to clear.
2. Backend: Modify the clear endpoint to accept a list of tables (or "groups") to clear, and only truncate those (in correct order to respect FKs, or use CASCADE for selected tables).
3. Frontend: Build an interactive UI with dropdown/selection of tables, a schematic view (could be a simple tree or list with indentation showing dependencies), and explanations about cascade. When user selects tables, show what will be cascade-deleted.

Let me think about the schema:
- We have tables with FK dependencies. When we TRUNCATE table A CASCADE, it also truncates tables that have FK to A.
- So the "dependency" direction for display: if B has FK to A, then B depends on A. When we clear "leads", we need to clear intent_signals, score_history, deals, etc. first (children) or use CASCADE (then truncating leads will cascade to children).
- For an interactive UI we need:
  1. List of clearable tables with human-readable names and descriptions
  2. For each table: what other tables reference it (so if we clear this, we might want to clear those too - or CASCADE does it)
  3. For each table: what tables it references (dependencies - we need to clear those first or CASCADE handles order)

Actually the simpler model:
- "Groups" or individual tables. User selects which "areas" to clear, e.g. "Leads and all related", "Deals", "Events", "Campaigns", "Conversations & messages", "Automation", "Email sequences", "Organizations". Each group maps to a set of tables. When clearing a group we truncate in dependency order or use CASCADE.
- Or: show a tree of tables. Root nodes: organizations, campaigns, pipelines (we don't clear), leads, etc. Under leads: intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations -> messages, email_sequence_enrollments -> email_tracking. So the tree shows "if you clear X, you'll also clear Y, Z (children)". User selects nodes to clear.

I'll go with:
1. Backend: GET /api/v1/admin/clear-databases/schema - returns list of "clearable groups" (or tables) with:
   - id, label, description
   - tables: string[] (table names to truncate when this group is selected)
   - depends_on: group ids that must be cleared first (or we clear in order)
   - cascade_effect: human-readable "При очистке также будут удалены: ..."

   Or we could return a graph: nodes = tables (or groups), edges = FK relationships. Then frontend can render a diagram and let user select nodes.

2. Backend: POST /api/v1/admin/clear-databases with body { groups: string[] } or { tables: string[] }. Server clears only those groups/tables in correct order.

3. Frontend: 
   - Dropdown or multi-select for "что очистить" (groups)
   - A schematic view: boxes for each group, arrows showing dependencies (A -> B means B references A, so clearing A might require clearing B first, or CASCADE)
   - When user selects groups, show "Будет выполнено: TRUNCATE ... CASCADE для: ..." and "Каскадно удалятся: ..."
   - Button "Очистить выбранное"

Let me define groups that make sense:

1. **organizations** - Организации. Каскад: ничего (или leads reference them - so we'd clear leads first in practice; actually TRUNCATE organizations CASCADE would truncate leads etc. that reference it. So order: clear children first, then parents. So we want to show: "Если очистить Organizations, то связи в Leads будут обнулены или удалены" - actually in our schema leads.organization_id REFERENCES organizations(id), so if we TRUNCATE organizations CASCADE, PostgreSQL will truncate leads too (because of FK from leads to organizations). So the cascade is: truncating organizations truncates leads (and everything that references leads). So the "dependency graph" for CASCADE: if B has FK to A, then truncating A CASCADE also truncates B. So we need to show: when you truncate "Organizations", PostgreSQL will also truncate: Leads (and then everything that references Leads), etc. So the order of truncation when we have multiple groups: we should truncate in "reverse dependency" order - first tables that no one references (leaf tables), then work up. Or we can truncate all selected in one TRUNCATE ... CASCADE.

   So if user selects [organizations, campaigns], we run TRUNCATE organizations, campaigns RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE. But wait - leads references organizations. So TRUNCATE organizations CASCADE will truncate leads. And deals reference leads, so leads CASCADE will truncate deals. So we need to list for the user: "При очистке Organizations будут каскадно очищены: leads, intent_signals, score_history, deals, ..." 

   I'll define groups as sets of "root" tables. When user selects a group, we truncate that table (and CASCADE does the rest). So:
   - **organizations** - Организации. Каскадно: leads → intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations, messages, email_sequence_enrollments, ...
   - **campaigns** - Кампании. Каскадно: (nothing else references campaigns in our schema? Let me check - events has campaign_id but it's not FK to campaigns table in initial schema - it's just VARCHAR). So campaigns is standalone.
   - **leads** - Лиды. Каскадно: intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations, messages, email_sequence_enrollments (and email_tracking, email_sequence_steps? No - email_sequence_steps references email_sequences; email_sequence_enrollments references leads. So leads CASCADE: intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations->messages, email_sequence_enrollments->email_tracking). So if we truncate "leads" CASCADE we get a lot. 
   - **pipelines** - мы не очищаем по умолчанию
   - **deals** - Сделки. Каскадно: automation_logs (rule_id, lead_id, deal_id), tasks (deal_id), conversations (deal_id) -> messages. So deals CASCADE: tasks, automation_logs, conversations->messages.
   - **events** - События. Отдельная таблица (partitioned). Нет FK от других к events.
   - **scoring_rules** - не очищаем
   - **automation_rules** - Правила автоматизации. Каскад: automation_logs, tasks (task can reference automation_rule_id).
   - **conversations** - Разговоры. Каскад: messages.
   - **email_sequences** - Последовательности писем. Каскад: email_sequence_steps, email_sequence_enrollments -> email_tracking.

   So logical "groups" for the UI:
   1. **organizations** - Организации (компании лидов). Каскадно удалит лидов и всё связанное с ними.
   2. **campaigns** - Кампании.
   3. **leads** - Лиды (без организаций). Каскадно: intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations, messages, email_sequence_enrollments.
   4. **deals** - Сделки. Каскадно: tasks, automation_logs, conversations, messages.
   5. **events** - События (маркетинговые события).
   6. **automation_rules** - Правила автоматизации. Каскадно: automation_logs, tasks (те что по правилу).
   7. **conversations** - Чаты/разговоры. Каскадно: messages.
   8. **email_sequences** - Email-последовательности. Каскадно: steps, enrollments, tracking.

   But wait - if we let user select "leads" and "organizations" separately, and they select only "organizations", then we truncate organizations CASCADE and that will truncate leads anyway (FK). So we need to show that. So the schema endpoint should return:
   - nodes: list of clearable tables (or groups) with label, description
   - edges: from A to B meaning "B has FK to A" (so truncating A CASCADE truncates B). So when user selects "organizations", we show "каскадно удалятся: leads, intent_signals, ..." by traversing the graph.

   I'll implement:
   1. GET /admin/clear-databases/schema - returns:
      - groups: { id, label, description, tableName, cascadeTo: string[] }[]  (cascadeTo = tables that have FK to this table, so will be truncated when this is truncated with CASCADE)
   2. POST /admin/clear-databases - body: { groupIds: string[] }. Server truncates those tables (in safe order) with CASCADE.

   Actually "safe order" when multiple selected: we should truncate in topological order (dependencies first - i.e. tables that are referenced first... no). TRUNCATE T CASCADE truncates T and all tables that have a foreign key TO T. So if we have organizations and campaigns selected, we do TRUNCATE organizations, campaigns CASCADE. PostgreSQL will truncate organizations and campaigns and any tables that reference them. So order in the list doesn't matter for CASCADE - we just list the selected root tables. But we must not truncate a table that references another selected table without CASCADE... Actually when we TRUNCATE A, B CASCADE, Postgres truncates A and B and every table that has FK to A or B. So we're good.

   Let me define the schema response. I need the actual FK graph from the DB or hardcode it. Hardcode is simpler.

   Tables and their "referenced by" (who has FK to this table):
   - organizations <- leads
   - pipelines <- leads, pipeline_stages, deals, automation_rules (we don't clear pipelines)
   - pipeline_stages <- deals, automation_rules (we don't clear)
   - leads <- intent_signals, score_history, deals, conversations, email_sequence_enrollments, events
   - scoring_rules <- score_history (we don't clear scoring_rules)
   - automation_rules <- automation_logs, tasks
   - deals <- automation_logs, tasks, conversations
   - events <- (partitioned, no one references)
   - email_sequences <- email_sequence_steps, email_sequence_enrollments
   - email_sequence_steps <- email_sequence_enrollments (no - email_tracking references enrollment and step), email_tracking references email_sequence_enrollments and email_sequence_steps
   - email_sequence_enrollments <- email_tracking
   - conversations <- messages
   - team_members <- conversations (created_by_id), messages (sender_id), email_accounts (we don't clear team)

   So clearable "root" tables (tables that we allow to truncate as a group):
   - organizations (cascade: leads -> intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations->messages, email_sequence_enrollments->email_tracking)
   - campaigns
   - automation_rules (cascade: automation_logs, tasks)
   - conversations (cascade: messages)
   - email_sequences (cascade: email_sequence_steps, email_sequence_enrollments, email_tracking)
   - events
   - leads (cascade: intent_signals, score_history, deals, events, conversations->messages, email_sequence_enrollments->email_tracking) - but leads ref organizations and pipelines, so truncating leads CASCADE will only truncate tables that reference leads
   - deals (cascade: automation_logs, tasks, conversations->messages)

   So the groups we offer (as "roots" to truncate):
   1. organizations - "Организации (компании)"
   2. campaigns - "Кампании"
   3. leads - "Лиды"
   4. deals - "Сделки"
   5. events - "События (маркетинговые события)"
   6. automation_rules - "Правила автоматизации"
   7. conversations - "Чаты и переписка"
   8. email_sequences - "Email-последовательности"

   And for each we compute cascadeTo from the FK graph. I'll hardcode the graph in the backend and return it. Then frontend shows a diagram (e.g. boxes with arrows) and multi-select, with explanation text.
</details></think>

  - список рёбер «родитель → дочерняя таблица» (у дочерней есть внешний ключ на родителя).

- **GET `/api/v1/admin/clear-databases/schema`** — отдаёт:
  - `groups` (с полем `cascadeTo` для каждой группы),
  - `edges` (пары `from` → `to` для схемы),
  - `tableLabels` (человекочитаемые названия таблиц).

- **POST `/api/v1/admin/clear-databases`** — тело запроса: `{ groupIds?: string[] }`:
  - если передан непустой `groupIds` — очищаются только выбранные группы (со своими таблицами и каскадом);
  - если не передан или пустой — очищаются все очищаемые таблицы, как раньше.

### 2. Фронтенд (настройки → System Config)

- Кнопка **«Выбрать и очистить базы данных»** открывает диалог.
- В диалоге:
  - **Схема связей** — список рёбер в виде «Чип A → Чип B» (у B есть внешний ключ на A), с пояснением про каскад и сохранение нормализации.
  - **Выбор групп** — мультиселект (Autocomplete) по группам; у каждой группы показывается подпись и описание.
  - При выборе групп выводится **предупреждение**: «При очистке выбранного каскадно удалятся также: …» — список зависимых таблиц.
- Кнопки:
  - **«Очистить выбранное»** — видна только при выборе хотя бы одной группы; очищаются только выбранные группы (и их каскад).
  - **«Очистить всё»** — очищает все очищаемые таблицы (прежнее поведение).

Таблицы остаются нормализованными: очистка делается через `TRUNCATE ... CASCADE` в правильном наборе таблиц, а в UI явно показано, какие связи есть и что удалится по каскаду.

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is asking where to find the button we added - the "Выбрать и очистить базы данных" button for clearing databases. I need to tell them the navigation path in the app.

From what I implemented:
- The button is in Settings → System Config tab
- It's in the card "Очистка баз данных" and only visible for admin users (identity?.role === 'admin')

So the path is: Settings (страница настроек) → вкладка "System Config" → карточка с красной рамкой "Очистка баз данных" → кнопка "Выбрать и очистить базы данных"
</details></think>

---


Кнопка находится так:

1. Откройте **Settings** (Настройки) — в меню приложения пункт настроек.
2. Перейдите на вкладку **System Config** (пятая вкладка: Profile, Team Management, Integrations, Pipelines, **System Config**, Notifications, Appearance).
3. Прокрутите страницу вниз до блока с красной рамкой **«Очистка баз данных»**.
4. Там будет кнопка **«Выбрать и очистить базы данных»**.

Важно: этот блок и кнопка показываются **только пользователям с ролью Administrator** (`identity?.role === 'admin'`). Если вы залогинены как BDR, AE и т.п., карточки «Очистка баз данных» не будет. Войдите под учётной записью администратора.

The user is on the System Config tab and they don't see the "Выбрать и очистить базы данных" card. Looking at the HTML they pasted, I see:
- Routing Configuration
- Score Tiers
- Intent → Pipeline Mapping
- Feature Flags
- Scoring Thresholds

But there's NO card for "Очистка баз данных" (Clear databases). The card is only rendered when `isAdmin` is true:

```jsx
{isAdmin && (
  <Grid item xs={12}>
    <Card sx={{ borderColor: 'error.light', ... }}>
```

So either:
1. The user is not logged in as admin (identity?.role !== 'admin'), so the block is hidden.
2. Or there's something wrong with how we're rendering it - e.g. the card might be after "Feature Flags" and before "Scoring Thresholds" in the code. Let me check the order in SystemConfig.jsx.

Looking at my earlier edits, the "Clear databases" card was placed after "Feature Flags" and before "Scoring Thresholds Details". So in the DOM order it should appear after Feature Flags. In the user's HTML I see: Routing, Score Tiers, Intent→Pipeline, Feature Flags, Scoring Thresholds. So the Clear DB card is missing - which means `isAdmin` is false for them.

So the fix is: show the card to everyone (or at least show a message for non-admins), OR the user needs to log in as admin. The user said they can't find the component - so the most likely issue is that their account doesn't have role 'admin'. 

I have two options:
1. Tell the user they need to be admin and how to check/become admin.
2. Show the card to all users but disable the button or show "Only for administrators" for non-admins, so at least they see where it is.

I'll do option 2: always show the card so the user can find it, but for non-admins show a message that only administrators can use it (and disable the button or don't open the dialog with schema). That way the component is visible and they understand why it might not work.
</details></think>

Готово. Карточка **«Очистка баз данных»** теперь **всегда видна** на вкладке **System Config**.

Где она:
- Откройте **Settings** → вкладка **System Config**.
- Прокрутите вниз: после блоков **Routing Configuration**, **Score Tiers**, **Intent → Pipeline Mapping**, **Feature Flags** идёт карточка с красной рамкой **«Очистка баз данных»** и кнопкой **«Выбрать и очистить базы данных»**.

Если у вашего пользователя **не роль Administrator**, карточка всё равно отображается, но:
- сверху показывается предупреждение: «Доступно только для пользователей с ролью Administrator»;
- кнопка **неактивна** (disabled).

Чтобы кнопка работала, нужно войти под учётной записью с ролью **admin**. Роль задаётся в **Team Management** (Settings → Team Management) или при первом пользователе в системе (он создаётся как admin). Если нужно, могу подсказать, как проверить/поменять роль в коде или в БД.
